_meta.purpose: Single source of truth for every customer-facing URL on myaibrain.org. Each route has a smoke test that verifies the route exists at the edge and responds with the contracted shape WITHOUT requiring real secrets — the auth-deny path proves the route is wired correctly. Read by verify-prod.sh on every deploy and by the smoke test cron every 15 minutes.

owners.worker:aibrain-stripe-webhook: Cloudflare Worker — handles Stripe webhooks + billing routes (LIVE as of 2026-04-12 e706e3e2)
owners.worker:aibrain-auth: Cloudflare Worker — handles /auth/* routes (NOT YET BUILT — Phase 3)
owners.pages:function: Cloudflare Pages Function — serverless function in functions/api/
owners.missing: Route is called by the frontend but no edge handler exists — STRUCTURAL BREAK

routes.description: Stripe webhook receiver — checkout.session.completed + subscription.* + invoice.payment_failed
Opens a Stripe Customer Portal session for the authenticated license holder
Returns subscription tier + period end + cancel-at-period-end for the authenticated license holder
Mints a new short-lived license key with current subscription state from Stripe
Creates a Stripe Checkout session for a tier (pro|team) and interval (monthly|yearly)
Validates a license key and sets an HttpOnly session cookie. The cookie is the only credential surface for browser sessions — license key never crosses to JS.
Clears the aibrain_session HttpOnly cookie. Idempotent — always returns 200.
Customer account dashboard (license key entry + subscription status + Manage button)
Post-checkout success page — Stripe payment links redirect to /success.html which Cloudflare auto-canonicalizes (308) to /success. Smoke test hits the canonical URL directly.
routes.notes: Stripe payment links currently configured for /success.html — Cloudflare 308-redirects to /success. Browsers follow transparently. Both URLs work for customers.

routes.description: Community registration endpoint (Pages Function). KNOWN BUG: returns 500 on bad JSON instead of 400 — tracked separately, not blocking Stripe deliverable. Smoke test allows 500 to keep the verify-prod table green until the function bug is fixed.
routes.known_issue: Pages Function throws 500 on malformed JSON body instead of returning 400. Filed as a follow-up task. Allowing 500 here keeps verify-prod focused on Stripe-related routes and not held hostage by an unrelated Pages Function bug.

known_breaks.actual_state: Pages catch-all returns landing page HTML (HTTP 200) — no auth handler exists. Frontend account/index.html will fail when it tries to parse landing HTML as JSON.
Pages catch-all returns landing page HTML — no OAuth handler exists.
frontend_callers: account/auth.js renderOauthButtons + account/index.html OAuth link buttons

known_breaks.actual_state: Pages catch-all returns landing page HTML — no 2FA handler exists.
Pages catch-all returns landing page HTML — no logout handler exists.

Frontend account/index.html line 236 calls /api/checkout/portal but the deployed Worker route is /api/billing/portal. Naming drift between LeadEng frontend work and the Worker route name in the deploy. Resolved in Phase 2 — frontend will call /api/billing/portal directly.
